title: Wedelia serrulata
intro: Wedelia serrulata, also known as the green owl flower or yellow daisy, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potent health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia serrulata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Wedelia serrulata include infusions, decoctions, and powders, often used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Wedelia serrulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Heliantheae. The genus Wedelia is part of the larger group of plants known as daisies or sunflowers. Wedelia serrulata is native to tropical America and is often cultivated for its ornamental value. Its classification is also supported by its characteristics, which include its serrated leaves and bright yellow flowers.
